title: Remove Timing Pulley From Taper-Lock Bushing and Shaft
summary: Remove the timing pulley assembly from the shaft by locking out the tipper, gaining access to the motor area if needed, removing the taper-lock bushing set screws, using one set screw in the threaded release hole to free the pulley from the bushing, and sliding the assembly off the shaft.
when_to_use: Use when servicing or removing the timing pulley assembly and the documented source procedure calls for freeing the timing pulley from the taper-lock bushing and shaft using the set screw release method in section 7.3.19.1 Removal.
not_for: Do not use for operator-performed work.
Do not use hammering or impact removal of the pulley.
Do not use alternate removal methods not documented in the source when the pulley does not break free.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform LOTO, remove access panels if needed, remove and reinstall service hardware as applicable, free the pulley from the taper-lock bushing, and remove the pulley assembly from the shaft.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before beginning the procedure.
Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.
Do not hit the pulley with a hammer because the source states this will damage the gearbox bearings.
This procedure involves mechanical disassembly and is not operator-safe.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the station side panel and motor area

access_required: Access to the station side panel and motor area
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out and tagged out before service.
Motor area is accessible, including side panel removal if needed.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-20 timing pulley maintenance diagram showing set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and component relationship used during removal.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe service state before mechanical disassembly.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before performing any timing pulley removal work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in a locked out and tagged out condition and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: Energy isolation is established and the unit is ready for service work.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not fully locked out or safe access has not been established.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Safe maintenance condition cannot be established.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor area and timing pulley assembly.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and the motor area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The motor area and timing pulley assembly can be reached for service.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed or access to the motor area remains obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel screws
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing pulley assembly location and surrounding motor-area components used for access orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed without damage.
Required access to the motor area cannot be obtained.

steps.title: Remove both taper-lock bushing set screws
steps.purpose: Prepare the bushing for use of the threaded release hole.
steps.instruction: Use a 1/8-in. hex wrench to remove both set screws from the bushing. Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.
steps.expected_result: Both set screws are removed from the taper-lock bushing without damaging the screw heads.
steps.healthy_condition: Both set screws are fully removed and available for reuse in the release hole.
steps.failure_condition: A set screw cannot be removed or the screw head is damaged.

actions.label: Remove set screws
actions.exact_action: Use a 1/8-in. hex wrench to remove both set screws from the bushing.

actions.label: Avoid round hex end
actions.exact_action: Do not use the round end of the hex wrench.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Set screw locations on the timing pulley and taper-lock bushing assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: Either set screw cannot be removed.
The hex interface is damaged or begins to strip.

value.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text notes not using the round end of the hex wrench and the figure identifies the set screws.

steps.title: Identify the threaded release hole
steps.purpose: Locate the correct hole used to force the pulley free from the taper-lock bushing.
steps.instruction: Identify the hole between the two set screw holes that has threads on the taper-lock bushing side and is smooth on the pulley side.
steps.expected_result: The correct center release hole is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected hole is between the two set screw holes and matches the threaded-on-bushing-side, smooth-on-pulley-side description.
steps.failure_condition: The correct release hole cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Locate release hole
actions.exact_action: Identify the hole between the two set screw holes that has threads on the taper-lock bushing side and is smooth on the pulley side.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Relationship between the set screw holes, taper-lock bushing, and timing pulley to identify the center threaded release hole.

stop_or_escalate_if: The release hole cannot be positively identified.
Hole geometry does not match the source description.

steps.title: Thread a removed set screw into the release hole
steps.purpose: Set up the mechanical release action that separates the pulley from the taper-lock bushing.
steps.instruction: Thread one removed set screw into the identified center hole.
steps.expected_result: One removed set screw is installed in the release hole and ready to be tightened.
steps.healthy_condition: The set screw threads smoothly into the correct hole.
steps.failure_condition: The set screw does not thread into the hole or appears to be in the wrong location.

actions.label: Install release screw
actions.exact_action: Thread one removed set screw into that center hole.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Center release hole location relative to the two set screw holes.

stop_or_escalate_if: The set screw will not thread into the identified hole.
Thread engagement is not possible or appears incorrect.

steps.title: Tighten the release screw to free the pulley
steps.purpose: Break the pulley free from the taper-lock bushing using the documented release method.
steps.instruction: Tighten the set screw in the center release hole to break the pulley free from the taper-lock bushing. The source notes this may take some work to get the pulley free.
steps.expected_result: The pulley breaks free from the taper-lock bushing.
steps.healthy_condition: The pulley loosens from the bushing using the set screw release method.
steps.failure_condition: The pulley does not break free using the documented set screw method.

actions.label: Tighten release screw
actions.exact_action: Tighten the set screw to break the pulley free from the taper-lock bushing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Taper-lock bushing and timing pulley relationship while applying the release method.

stop_or_escalate_if: The pulley does not break free using the documented set screw method.
The assembly shows signs of damage during release.

steps.title: Assist removal by hand only if needed
steps.purpose: Allow manual assistance to complete separation without damaging the gearbox bearings.
steps.instruction: If needed, push or pull the pulley by hand, but do not hit the pulley with a hammer because the source states this will damage the gearbox bearings.
steps.expected_result: The pulley is moved free without impact damage.
steps.healthy_condition: The pulley can be assisted by hand and no hammering is used.
steps.failure_condition: The pulley still cannot be freed by the documented method and hand assistance, or impact force is being considered.

actions.label: Push or pull by hand
actions.exact_action: If needed, push or pull the pulley by hand.

actions.label: Do not hammer pulley
actions.exact_action: Do not hit the pulley with a hammer because this will damage the gearbox bearings.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pulley and gearbox relationship to avoid impact damage during final freeing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The pulley cannot be freed by hand after the documented release method.
Any removal approach would require striking the pulley.

steps.title: Slide the pulley assembly off the shaft
steps.purpose: Complete removal of the freed timing pulley assembly.
steps.instruction: Once the pulley is free, slide everything off the shaft.
steps.expected_result: The timing pulley assembly is removed from the shaft.
steps.healthy_condition: The freed pulley assembly slides off the shaft cleanly.
steps.failure_condition: The assembly does not slide off the shaft after being freed.

actions.label: Remove pulley assembly
actions.exact_action: Once the pulley is free, slide everything off the shaft.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing pulley assembly components and shaft relationship during final removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The assembly will not slide off the shaft after being freed.

success_criteria: The timing pulley is freed from the taper-lock bushing using the documented set screw release method.
The pulley assembly slides off the shaft.
No hammering or impact damage occurs during removal.
healthy_conditions: The tipper is locked out before service begins.
The correct center release hole is identified.
A removed set screw threads into the release hole.
The pulley breaks free from the taper-lock bushing.
The assembly slides off the shaft after release.
failure_conditions: The side panel cannot be removed when access is needed.
The set screws cannot be removed.
The correct release hole cannot be identified.
The removed set screw cannot be threaded into the release hole.
The pulley does not break free using the documented set screw method.
The assembly does not slide off the shaft after being freed.
Any attempt is made to strike the pulley with a hammer.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the pulley does not break free using the documented set screw method, because no additional removal method is provided in the source.
Stop and escalate if removal would require striking the pulley.
Escalate if the correct release hole cannot be identified from the source description and figure.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall timing pulley assembly, set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and component relationship used throughout the removal procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-20 appears on page 182 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_19_1_removal.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide the full text of the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
The source packet does not provide an estimated completion time for this removal procedure.
The source packet does not provide any additional removal method if the pulley does not break free using the documented set screw method.

The source packet lists tools including replacement pulley part, torque wrench, and 8-mm hex wrench, but this packet does not explicitly tie each of those items to a specific removal step in the provided subsection text.
